title: Chrysanthemum junnanicum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum junnanicum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics, which align with those of other plants within the Asteraceae family. More specifically, Chrysanthemum junnanicum is a member of the tribe Anthemideae, which includes other common flowering plants like chamomile and artemisia. The genus Chrysanthemum is also part of the larger classification of flowering plants called the angiosperms, or flowering plants that produce seeds within a fruit or flower. This comprehensive classification helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships between different plant species.
uses: Chrysanthemum junnanicum, a species of flowering plant, has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications in its native range. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant's flowers and leaves are often used in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal benefits. In traditional Chinese medicine, Chrysanthemum junnanicum is believed to help balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being. Further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

It is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties may help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. parts: Chrysanthemum junnanicum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine. preparations: Chrysanthemum junnanicum is used in traditional herbal preparations in various parts of Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties. side_effects: Chrysanthemum junnanicum is not typically considered for human consumption, but if ingested, it may cause gastrointestinal upset due to its potential toxicity. Some sources suggest that ingesting large quantities may lead to liver and kidney damage. Additionally, topical application of Chrysanthemum junnanicum may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of Chrysanthemum junnanicum.
